key_insight: MatMMExtract turns compound materials-science figures into panel-level, grounded multimodal data at scale, making the visual literature usable for AI.
engineering_relevance: The released MatSciFig and MaterialScope resources provide a practical foundation for training and evaluating retrieval, detection, and vision-language models in scientific imaging workflows.
method: Decomposed 180,571 compound figures from 14,810 open-access papers into individual sub-panels.
Used a curated materials-science taxonomy and an LLM to generate sub-captions, visual categories, and scientific summaries.
Built MaterialScope for panel localization and fine-tuned YOLO12-m for detection.
evidence_signal: MatSciFig contains 391,606 panel-level image-text pairs, MaterialScope has 2,811 manually annotated figures, and the detector reached mAP_50 of 0.9227 while Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite produced 82% good outputs with 4.8% hallucinations.
watch_item: LLM-generated annotations may still inherit domain or taxonomy biases, so downstream users should validate labels for high-stakes or highly specialized applications.
paper_title: Unlocking the Visual Record of Materials Science: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset from Scientific Literature
